name: Russian anti-war movement (white-blue-white)

colors.symbolism: the outer stripes — peace, and a Russia without the red of war and authoritarianism
the central blue stripe — historic Russian heraldic blue retained from the tricolour

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — white, blue, white — derived from the Russian national tricolour by replacing both red bands with white.
history: The white-blue-white flag emerged in early March 2022 as a symbol of the Russian anti-war movement opposing Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. It was proposed in Telegram channels almost simultaneously by several authors (notably the writer Kai Katonina and the journalist Marina Litvinovich), the unifying reading being a Russian tricolour 'cleansed' of the red commonly associated with imperial militarism and authoritarianism. The flag spread rapidly among Russian opposition emigrés, anti-war protests abroad, and the Free Russia movement; the Russian government has prosecuted its display in Russia.

features.symbolism: the Russian tricolour with both red bands replaced by white — peace replacing war
